The constructor takes the number of nodes and the embedding dimensionality, and initializes a Parameter tensor of shape (num_nodes, embedding_dim). The forward() method returns the complete embedding matrix. In the test block, we instantiate the class, retrieve embeddings, and demonstrate a simple optimization step to confirm that embeddings are trainable. This component can be plugged into larger models requiring per-node representations, such as graph-structured diffusion architectures.

techniques.name: Array Embeddings (AE)
techniques.description: Group nodes that form an ordered array (e.g. entries of a matrix) and assign (a) a shared ‘array embedding’ per array, plus (b) a sinusoidal positional embedding per node index within that array. The final node embedding is the sum of the array embedding and its positional embedding. Enables networks to generalize to variable array sizes.

techniques.name: Exchangeable Embeddings (EE)
techniques.description: Enforce exact permutation invariances by sharing the same embedding for all nodes in a symmetry class A. By Theorem: if the sparse attention mask M satisfies M=πM for any permutation π on indices A, then sharing embeddings across A makes the learned pθ invariant to π. This allows the model to honor plate-style symmetry (e.g. row/column permutations in matrix factorization) without over-constraining other dependencies.

code.implementation:
import torch
from torch import nn
from typing import Sequence, Optional


class ExchangeableEmbeddings(nn.Module):
    """
    Enforce exact permutation invariances by sharing the same embedding for all nodes
    in a symmetry class. Given a class_map that assigns each variable index to a class,
    this module learns one embedding per class and looks up embeddings for each variable.
    """

    def __init__(self, class_map: Sequence[int], embed_dim: int) -> None:
        """
        Initialize the ExchangeableEmbeddings module.

        Parameters:
        - class_map (Sequence[int]): A sequence of length n_vars mapping each variable index
          to a class identifier. Variables with the same class share the same embedding.
        - embed_dim (int): Dimension of each embedding vector.
        """
        super().__init__()
        # compress class IDs into contiguous range [0, num_classes)
        orig_classes = list(class_map)
        unique_classes = sorted(set(orig_classes))
        remap = [unique_classes.index(c) for c in orig_classes]
        self.register_buffer('class_map_tensor', torch.tensor(remap, dtype=torch.long))
        self.num_classes = len(unique_classes)
        self.embed_dim = embed_dim

        # one embedding vector per class
        self.embeddings = nn.Parameter(
            torch.randn(self.num_classes, embed_dim),
            requires_grad=True
        )

    def forward(self, batch_size: Optional[int] = None) -> torch.Tensor:
        """
        Get embeddings for each variable, optionally repeated over a batch.

        Parameters:
        - batch_size (Optional[int]): If provided, the returned tensor will have shape
          (batch_size, n_vars, embed_dim) by repeating the variable embeddings across
          the batch dimension. If None, returns shape (n_vars, embed_dim).

        Returns:
        - torch.Tensor: Embeddings tensor of shape (n_vars, embed_dim) or
          (batch_size, n_vars, embed_dim).
        """
        # lookup embeddings by class
        var_embeds = self.embeddings[self.class_map_tensor]  # (n_vars, embed_dim)
        if batch_size is not None:
            # expand to batch dimension
            var_embeds = var_embeds.unsqueeze(0).expand(batch_size, -1, -1)
        return var_embeds
code.test:
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Example: a problem with 7 variables belonging to two symmetry classes: 0 and 1.
    class_map = [0, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1, 1]  # first 3 vars in class 0; next 4 in class 1
    embed_dim = 8
    batch_size = 2

    ee = ExchangeableEmbeddings(class_map, embed_dim)
    # get per-variable embeddings without batch
    var_embs = ee()                  # shape: (7, 8)
    print("Variable embeddings shape:", var_embs.shape)

    # get embeddings for a batch of size 2
    batched_embs = ee(batch_size)    # shape: (2, 7, 8)
    print("Batched embeddings shape:", batched_embs.shape)

    # verify that embeddings within each class are identical
    assert torch.allclose(var_embs[0], var_embs[1])
    assert torch.allclose(var_embs[3], var_embs[4])
    print("Exchangeability check passed.")
code.documentation: This module implements Exchangeable Embeddings (EE) by assigning each variable index to a symmetry class and learning one embedding vector per class. The class_map input defines which variables share embeddings, and arbitrary class labels are compressed into a contiguous range internally. On forward pass, embeddings for each variable are looked up and optionally expanded across a batch dimension. This enforces exact permutation invariance within each symmetry class, as variables in the same class always receive the same embedding. The test block demonstrates usage and verifies that variables assigned to the same class have identical embeddings.

techniques.name: Intermediate Variables
techniques.description: Include latent ‘intermediate’ nodes—introduced during graphical model specification to make dependencies explicit—in the diffusion model’s target x0, rather than omitting them. This provides additional supervised signal per training example, dramatically reducing required data and training time and can reduce sparse attention density when the graph is sparser in its expanded form.

techniques.name: Flexible Conditioning
techniques.description: Distinguish observed vs. latent nodes at training and inference by adding a learned ‘observation embedding’ vector to tokens corresponding to observed variables y, while latent tokens xt receive no such offset. At test time this allows arbitrary subsets of variables to be observed or missing without re-architecting the network.

code.implementation:
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch import Tensor
from typing import Optional, Tuple, Union

class FlexibleConditioning(nn.Module):
    """
    Implements flexible conditioning by distinguishing observed vs. latent tokens.
    Observed tokens are replaced with their ground-truth values and receive
    an added learned observation embedding offset; latent tokens remain unchanged.
    """
    def __init__(self, emb_dim: int):
        """
        Initialize the FlexibleConditioning module.

        Parameters:
            emb_dim (int): Dimensionality of the token embeddings.
        """
        super().__init__()
        # Learned observation embedding of shape (1, 1, emb_dim)
        self.cond_emb = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(1, 1, emb_dim))

    def forward(
        self,
        x: Tensor,
        obs: Tensor,
        obs_mask_xt: Tensor,
        emb: Optional[Tensor] = None,
        obs_mask_emb: Optional[Tensor] = None
    ) -> Union[Tensor, Tuple[Tensor, Tensor]]:
        """
        Apply flexible conditioning to input tokens and optionally to their embeddings.

        This performs:
            x_cond = x * (1 - obs_mask_xt) + obs * obs_mask_xt

        and if embeddings are provided:
            emb_cond = emb + cond_emb * obs_mask_emb

        Parameters:
            x (Tensor): Latent token tensor of shape (B, N).
            obs (Tensor): Observed token tensor of shape (B, N).
            obs_mask_xt (Tensor): Binary mask (0 or 1) of shape (B, N) indicating
                                  which tokens are observed (1) vs. latent (0).
            emb (Tensor, optional): Token embeddings of shape (B, N, D).
            obs_mask_emb (Tensor, optional): Embedding mask of shape broadcastable
                                             to (B, N, D), typically same as
                                             obs_mask_xt.unsqueeze(-1).

        Returns:
            If emb and obs_mask_emb are provided, returns a tuple (x_cond, emb_cond):
                x_cond (Tensor): Conditioned tokens of shape (B, N).
                emb_cond (Tensor): Conditioned embeddings of shape (B, N, D).
            Otherwise, returns:
                x_cond (Tensor): Conditioned tokens of shape (B, N).
        """
        # Substitute observed values into x
        x_cond = x * (1.0 - obs_mask_xt) + obs * obs_mask_xt

        # If embeddings are provided, add the learned observation embedding
        if emb is not None and obs_mask_emb is not None:
            emb_cond = emb + self.cond_emb * obs_mask_emb
            return x_cond, emb_cond

        return x_cond
code.test:
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Example usage and simple test case
    B, N, D = 2, 5, 4  # batch size, number of tokens, embedding dimension
    torch.manual_seed(0)

    # Instantiate the conditioning module
    flex_cond = FlexibleConditioning(emb_dim=D)

    # Create dummy latent tokens, observations, and masks
    x = torch.randn(B, N)
    obs = torch.randn(B, N)
    obs_mask_xt = torch.randint(0, 2, (B, N)).float()  # 1 => observed, 0 => latent

    # Create dummy embeddings and corresponding mask
    emb = torch.randn(B, N, D)
    obs_mask_emb = obs_mask_xt.unsqueeze(-1).expand(B, N, D)

    # Apply flexible conditioning
    x_cond, emb_cond = flex_cond(x, obs, obs_mask_xt, emb, obs_mask_emb)

    # Verify shapes and basic correctness
    assert x_cond.shape == (B, N), "Conditioned tokens should have shape (B, N)"
    assert emb_cond.shape == (B, N, D), "Conditioned embeddings should have shape (B, N, D)"
    # At positions where obs_mask_xt==1, x_cond must equal obs
    observed_positions = obs_mask_xt.bool()
    assert torch.allclose(x_cond[observed_positions], obs[observed_positions]), \
        "Observed positions in x_cond must match obs"
    print("FlexibleConditioning test passed. x_cond and emb_cond shapes:", x_cond.shape, emb_cond.shape)
code.documentation:
This module implements “Flexible Conditioning,” a technique to handle arbitrary
observed subsets of variables at both training and inference without
re-architecting the network. The class holds a learnable observation embedding
vector, which is added only to token embeddings corresponding to observed
variables. The forward pass substitutes observed values into the latent token
vector and, if embeddings are provided, offsets the embeddings at observed
locations. By toggling the binary observation masks, users can flexibly mask
or reveal any subset of tokens. The emb_dim parameter controls the size of the
learned embedding. The included test block demonstrates basic correctness
and shape verification.

techniques.name: Mixed Discrete/Continuous Handling
techniques.description: Map discrete variables to continuous one-hot vectors before diffusion. Diffuse these one-hot encodings in Rk, embed each full vector into a single transformer token, and at generation time map back to discrete values by argmax. This lets a single diffusion backbone handle both discrete factors (e.g. Sudoku cells, permutation matrices) and continuous factors (e.g. real-valued matrices) jointly.

techniques.name: Efficient Sparse Attention Implementation
techniques.description: An O(n·m)-time implementation of the sparsified self-attention. Given n tokens and a mask M with at most m nonzero entries per row, build packed tensors K',V' of shape n×m×d by gathering each token’s allowed keys and values. Compute unnormalized scores U of shape n×m via U[i,j]=Q[i]·K'[i,j], mask out padding positions to –∞, then apply softmax row-wise and finally multiply by V' to obtain the output embeddings. Each step scales linearly in n·m instead of n^2.
